meals.name: Easy Shrimp Alfredo
meals.label: Meal A
ings: 1 pound uncooked shrimp (31-40/pound size, thawed & peeled)
4 ounces cream cheese (softened, cut into smaller pieces)
steps: Boil a large salted pot of water for fettuccine. Cook al dente according to package directions.
Add butter, cream cheese, cream, chicken broth, and garlic to a skillet over medium heat. Once butter melts and pan is hot, help cream cheese melt into the sauce. Takes a couple of minutes.
When cream cheese is incorporated, let it gently bubble for 5 minutes until sauce has reduced somewhat.
Stir in parmesan. Let cook for about 1 minute.
Add shrimp to the pan. Cook for 5–6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Season with salt & pepper. Toss with drained pasta and serve immediately.
meals.note: 💡 If sauce gets too thick, thin it out with a bit of hot pasta water before draining.

meals.name: Butter Cauliflower
meals.label: Meal B
ings: 1 medium head of cauliflower, cut into florets
Pinch of cayenne (optional — skip for kids)
½ cup heavy cream or full-fat coconut milk
Fresh cilantro and Greek yogurt for garnish
steps: Combine lemon juice, cornstarch, cumin, ½ tsp each of turmeric, garam masala, and salt in a large bowl. Set aside.

Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Cook cauliflower, tossing occasionally, until browned and beginning to soften, 7–8 minutes. Reduce heat to medium, transfer cauliflower to cornstarch mixture and toss to coat. Return to skillet and cook another 7–8 minutes until charred in spots. Transfer to a bowl.

Add remaining oil and butter. Add onion and cook until translucent, ~8 minutes. Stir in garlic, ginger, and tomato paste; cook 2 minutes. Add remaining turmeric, garam masala, paprika, cinnamon (and cayenne for adults); cook 1 minute.
Add tomato sauce and broth; bring to a boil. Reduce to simmer, stir in cream, and return cauliflower. Simmer uncovered until sauce thickens, ~15 minutes.
Serve over basmati rice. Garnish with cilantro and a dollop of Greek yogurt.
meals.note: 💡 Skip cayenne entirely and keep it mild — great for the whole family. The double-cook on the cauliflower (cornstarch coating) is the secret — don't skip it.

meals.name: Creamy Tomato Gnocchi with Burrata
meals.label: Meal C
ings: 6 to 8 garlic cloves, peeled and lightly crushed
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes (optional — skip for kids)
1/2 cup fresh basil leaves, sliced or torn
steps: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low. Add garlic and cook until fragrant, about 2 minutes.

Increase heat to medium and add tomatoes. Cook, stirring occasionally, until most tomatoes break down and become jammy, 10–12 minutes. Season with salt and pepper (skip red pepper flakes for a kid-friendly version). Reduce heat to medium-low and stir in heavy cream.
Cook gnocchi according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve ½ cup pasta cooking water and drain remaining.
Transfer cooked gnocchi and reserved pasta water to skillet with tomato sauce. Toss continuously until sauce is thickened and glossy, about 2 minutes. Stir in fresh basil.
Break burrata into pieces and disperse evenly over pasta. Remove from heat and serve immediately.
meals.note: 💡 Don't heat the burrata — add it after. It melts slightly into the sauce and is incredible. Skip red pepper flakes and this is totally kid-friendly.
